title: Agentic Discovery of Cryomicroneedle Formulations
core_claim: An AI-assisted closed-loop workflow combining literature curation, surrogate modeling, Bayesian optimization, and sequential wet-lab validation can efficiently discover cryoprotectant formulations for cryomicroneedles with improved post-thaw viability.
triage_rationale: This is a concrete domain workflow paper, not a benchmark-only study: it includes iterative wet-lab validation, measurable gains over rounds, and an explicit materials/formulation discovery loop. The evidence in the abstract is strong enough to merit full review because the claims are tied to experimental outcomes, though the multi-objective limitation suggests some caution.
likely_red_flags: Prospective performance initially failed before iterative correction, so generalization appears fragile.
High viability did not guarantee intact device formation, indicating the objective may be only partially solved.
The abstract emphasizes agentic infrastructure, but the core contribution may be the closed-loop optimization pipeline rather than a broadly general agent method.
